finding: CRITICAL: `record.session.summary` is a stats object `{additions, deletions, files}`, NOT a text string. The plan's fuzzysort concatenation `${record.session.summary ?? ""}` will render as `[object Object]` in the search target. Additionally, the OpenAPI Session schema has NO text `summary` field — only the stats object. The plan must either remove `session.summary` from the search target or find the actual text summary field (which doesn't exist in the current schema).
repair_path: Remove `${record.session.summary ?? ""}` from the fuzzysort concatenation. Search only on `title` and `projectName`. If a text summary is needed, it must be added to the Session schema and server endpoint first.

finding: CRITICAL: `session.model` is an object `{ id: string, providerID: string, variant: string }` per the OpenAPI spec, NOT a string. The plan treats it as a simple string for comparison (`record.session.model !== state.modelFilter`) and uses it directly in `uniqueModels` memo. This will fail both at type-level (missing from generated TS types) and at runtime (object !== string compare always false).
repair_path: Compare against `record.session.model?.id` or serialize to a string like `"${model.providerID}/${model.id}"`. Update the `uniqueModels` memo to extract a string identifier from the model object. Add type augmentation or cast for the missing model field on the Session type.

finding: CRITICAL: The generated TypeScript `Session` type (`@opencode-ai/sdk/v2/client`) does NOT include `agent` or `model` fields, even though the OpenAPI spec defines them. The plan's references to `record.session.agent` and `record.session.model` will produce TypeScript errors. The generated types appear stale relative to the OpenAPI spec. The plan must regen the SDK types or add type assertions/augmentations.
repair_path: Either (a) run `./packages/sdk/js/script/build.ts` to regenerate SDK types before implementation, or (b) create a local type augmentation in home.tsx that extends the Session type with `agent?: string` and `model?: { id: string; providerID: string; variant: string }`, or (c) use `as` casts. Option (a) is preferred; add `./packages/sdk/js/script/build.ts` to the validation steps.

finding: The plan description says "Replace String.includes() with fuzzysort.go()" but the code uses `fuzzysort.single()`. While `fuzzysort.single` works for a single concatenated target, `fuzzysort.go` would search multiple targets separately and return the best result, which is more accurate. The approach of using single concatenated string loses field-level scoring (hits in title should rank higher than in projectName).
repair_path: Use `fuzzysort.go(value, [record.session.title, record.projectName], { threshold: -10000 })` and check `result.total > 0` instead of concatenating all fields into one string. This gives better per-field relevance scoring.

finding: Old sessions may not have `agent` or `model` fields populated (sessions created before these fields were added to the server). When a specific agent/model filter is active, sessions without these fields will be silently excluded with no indication to the user. The "All" default masks this, but if a user selects a specific agent, old sessions disappear without explanation.
repair_path: Add a visual indicator or count showing "N sessions without agent data" or include a fallback category like "Unknown" in the agent/model filter radio groups. At minimum, document this behavior.

finding: No test strategy is specified. The plan only mentions manual visual checks and `bun test --filter "home"` if home tests exist. The filter logic (4 combined filters applied as a pipeline) has enough combinatorial complexity to warrant at least a unit test for the filter function itself, especially given the dirty file nature of home.tsx.
repair_path: Extract the filter pipeline into a pure function `filterSessions(records, search, timeFilter, agentFilter, modelFilter)` and write a small test file `home.test.ts` with 5-10 cases covering: text search with fuzzysort, time filter combinations, agent filter, model filter, combined filter, empty states, missing agent/model fields.

finding: Objection 1: session.summary is a stats object {additions, deletions, files, diffs} not a text string. Plan searches on `${record.session.summary ?? ""}` which produces '[object Object]'.
repair_path: Remove session.summary from fuzzysort concatenation. Search only on title + projectName.

finding: Objection 2: session.model is an object {id, providerID, variant} not a string. Direct string comparison always returns false. uniqueModels treats it as string value.
repair_path: Compare against record.session.model?.id or serialize as `${providerID}/${id}`. Update uniqueModels to extract string identifier from object.

finding: Objection 3: session.agent and session.model missing from generated TypeScript types in sdk/js/src/gen/types.gen.ts even though OpenAPI schema defines them. TypeScript errors on record.session.agent.
repair_path: Regenerate SDK types via packages/sdk/js/script/build.ts, OR add local type augmentation. Add build step to validation.

finding: BLOCKER: Filter dropdown options truncated to first 15 sessions. uniqueAgents/uniqueModels computed from records() which includes .slice(0, HOME_SESSION_LIMIT). Agents beyond the 15th session invisible in filter dropdown.
repair_path: Derive uniqueAgents/uniqueModels from unfiltered session pool (before slice). Use the full session list for filter option discovery.

finding: MAJOR: Debounce clear race condition. onClear() calls setState('search', '') but does not call debouncedSetSearch.clear(). If user clears within 200ms of typing, debounce timer re-applies previous query.
